paragraphs.en: For a long time, scientists have wondered why the human brain is so exceptionally large. According to the "Machiavellian Intelligence Hypothesis," a widely accepted theory in evolutionary biology, our brain size did not merely evolve to invent primitive tools or survive an environmental menace like harsh weather. Instead, it evolved to manage complex social relationships. Early human ancestors relied heavily on group cohesion to survive. Living in large tribes, however, meant individuals had to constantly compete with their own group members for limited food and resources.
paragraphs.zh: 长期以来，科学家一直在追问：为什么人类的大脑会如此异常地exceptionally庞大？根据进化生物学中广为接受的“马基雅维利式Machiavellian智能假说”，我们的大脑并不只是为了发明原始工具，或在严酷天气这类环境威胁menace下生存而进化出来的。相反，它更主要是为了应对复杂的社会关系。早期人类祖先高度依赖群体凝聚力cohesion才能存活。然而，生活在大型部落里也意味着，个体必须持续与同群成员竞争有限的食物与资源。

paragraphs.en: In this competitive environment, deception became an incredibly powerful survival tool. While the idea of lying to one's friends might sound sinister, the ability to trick others was a crucial cognitive mutation. To process these complex social calculations, the human brain had to mutate and expand over generations. Instead of resorting to physical violence or destructive acts like arson to destroy rivals, early humans learned to manipulate their peers. This allowed them to gain resources without directly having to infringe upon the physical safety of the group. A notable outcome of this evolutionary process is that humans became experts at both creating lies and detecting them.
paragraphs.zh: 在这样的竞争环境里，欺骗deception逐渐成为一种极其强大的生存工具。虽然对朋友撒谎听上去多少有些阴险sinister，但欺骗他人的能力其实是一种关键的认知cognitive突变mutation。为了处理这些复杂的社会计算，人类大脑不得不在代际演化中不断扩张。早期人类并没有诉诸于resort to肢体暴力，或以纵火arson这类破坏性手段去消灭对手，而是学会了操纵自己的同伴。这样一来，他们就能在不直接侵犯infringe群体人身安全的前提下获取资源。这一进化过程带来的一个显著结果是：人类既擅长制造谎言，也擅长识破谎言。

paragraphs.en: Of course, if everyone lied constantly, society would collapse. To maintain a steady social structure, human cultures developed strong moral systems. From a young age, parents strive to inculcate the value of honesty in their children and heavily criticize dishonest behavior. These social rules help individuals control their selfish urge to cheat. However, deception did not disappear; it simply became more sophisticated. Today, a person might use a stylish appearance to look naturally trustworthy, or a politician might use convincing words to enlist the support of millions, hiding their true intentions behind a charming smile.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，如果每个人都不断撒谎，社会很快就会崩溃。为了维持稳定的社会结构，人类文化逐渐发展出强有力的道德体系。从小，父母就努力向孩子灌输inculcate诚实的价值，并严厉批评不诚实的行为。这些社会规范帮助个体压制自己自私的冲动urge，不至于轻易作弊。然而，欺骗并没有消失，它只是变得更加精致、更加隐蔽。今天，一个人可能会借助时髦得体的外表让自己看起来自然可信；政治人物也可能借助极具说服力的话语去争取enlist数百万人的支持，并把真实意图隐藏在一个迷人的微笑之后。

paragraphs.en: This constant, invisible battle between trickery and truth can bewilder sociologists, yet it has an unexpectedly beautiful side. The exact same brain circuits that allow us to tell a lie also allow us to imagine things that do not exist. Therefore, when we enjoy our leisure time reading a fiction novel or watching a movie, we are actually engaging with a form of harmless, consensual deception. Without the evolutionary need to deceive, human art and storytelling might never have existed.
paragraphs.zh: 这种诡计与真相之间持续不断、却又难以察觉的较量，确实会让社会学家感到困惑bewilder，但它也有一种出人意料的美感。因为正是那些让我们能够说谎的大脑回路，也让我们具备了想象不存在之物的能力。所以，当我们在闲暇leisure时光里阅读虚构作品fiction或观看电影时，其实正在参与一种无害且双方默许的consensual欺骗。如果没有进化中对欺骗能力的需求，人类的艺术与叙事传统也许根本不会诞生。

Machiavellian.def: Using clever, often manipulative methods to gain power or advantage.
Machiavellian.trans: 马基雅维利主义的，擅长权谋的

cohesion.def: The action or state of forming a united whole.

deception.def: The act of making someone believe something that is not true.

sinister.def: Giving the impression that something harmful or evil is happening.

cognitive.def: Relating to thinking, understanding, learning, and remembering.

mutation.def: A change in genes or structure that can produce new characteristics.

resort to.def: To use or adopt a method, especially an extreme one, because other options are unavailable.

arson.def: The criminal act of deliberately setting fire to property.

infringe.def: To limit or violate something such as a right, freedom, or rule.

inculcate.def: To teach an idea, attitude, or habit by repeated instruction.

urge.def: A strong desire or impulse to do something.

enlist.def: To obtain help, support, or participation from someone.

leisure.def: Time when you are not working and can relax or do things you enjoy.

fiction.def: Literature that describes imaginary events and people.

consensual.def: Agreed to by all the people involved.
consensual.trans: 双方同意的，两厢情愿的

chart.title: Two Survival Strategies: Force vs. Deception
chart.description: A theoretical comparison inspired by the passage, showing why strategic deception could outperform direct aggression in social groups while still requiring moral rules to keep society stable.

insights.title: Social Intelligence Pressure
insights.text: The passage suggests that managing alliances, trust, and deception placed stronger pressure on brain growth than simple environmental danger.

insights.title: Morality as a Counterweight
insights.text: Because widespread lying would destroy cooperation, human cultures had to build strong norms that reward honesty and punish cheating.

insights.title: Creativity as a Byproduct
insights.text: The same cognitive machinery that supports lying may also support fiction, storytelling, and other imaginative arts.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct heading for Paragraph C from the list of headings below.

headings.text: The biological reasons for larger brain sizes
How societies manage the instinct to lie
The destructive consequences of physical violence
Why modern politicians always tell the truth

rationale.en: Paragraph C explains that societies use moral education and social criticism to control dishonest impulses and preserve stability.
rationale.zh: 【出题者意图】：测试你是否能概括段落主旨。段落 C 的核心在于说明社会为了维持稳定，如何通过道德教育与社会规范来管理撒谎本能，并让这种本能以更隐蔽、更高级的方式存在。

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for the answer.
questions.contentTemplate: According to the Machiavellian Intelligence Hypothesis, early humans developed larger brains to handle complicated {{blank1}} rather than just environmental threats.

rationale.en: Paragraph A states that the brain evolved to manage 'complex social relationships' rather than only environmental dangers.
rationale.zh: 【出题者意图】：测试语法结构敏感度及同义替换。题干中的 handle 对应原文的 manage，environmental threats 对应 environmental menace，答案直接来自 Paragraph A 的 social relationships。

questions.statement: Modern politicians are generally better at using deception than our early human ancestors.

rationale.en: The passage mentions both early humans and modern politicians using deception, but it never compares which group is better at it.
rationale.zh: 【出题者意图】：测试对“事实边界”的精准把握。原文提到了现代政客会用语言争取支持，也提到了早期人类会进行欺骗，但没有进行跨时代的能力比较，因此只能选 Not Given。

questions.prompt: Which paragraph contains the following information?

items.statement: An explanation of how people can use their physical appearance as a deceptive strategy.

rationale.en: Paragraph C says that a person may use a stylish appearance to seem naturally trustworthy.
rationale.zh: 【出题者意图】：测试高强度搜索与意义转述。题干中的 physical appearance 对应 Paragraph C 中的 stylish appearance，定位非常明确。

questions.prompt: What is the writer's main point regarding human art in Paragraph D?

options.text: Art is primarily used by scientists to understand how the brain works.
Engaging with art is a beneficial byproduct of our evolutionary ability to lie.
Modern movies and novels cause people to become bewildered during their free time.
Storytelling is a form of dangerous deception that threatens social cohesion.

rationale.en: Paragraph D argues that the same brain circuits used for deception also make fiction, art, and storytelling possible.
rationale.zh: 【出题者意图】：测试深层理解与排除法。Paragraph D 明确指出，让我们能够撒谎的大脑回路，同样让我们能够想象不存在的事物，因此艺术可以被看作进化出欺骗能力后的一个无害副产品。
